reviews.review_description:
4 major titles on Quest from Lucas and ILM, and in NONE of them, do you fly an X-Wing around the Death Star shooting Tie Fighters. A game that would sell itself. 

Instead, we get this cartoonish dribble which, bafflingly, doesn't put you in the driving seat of a Podracer, which would have worked amazingly well in VR.

I'm starting to think the head of ILM VR is an actual plank of wood. 

Star Wars in VR is as lost and directionless as Star Wars onscreen.

It’s a good game you can kind of touch whatever you want In one level but the Pod racing is really hard. It was my least favorite part. Besides that when you were in not half reality, it was very fun I would get this game if you’re a huge Star Wars fan like me.￼ It also Has you fixing￼ your pod, so I’ll like that￼.

Star Wars: Beyond Victory honestly blew me away, and that’s exactly why it feels so frustratingly underused.

The atmosphere, presentation, and especially the holographic table concept are incredible. Playing the races as if you’re controlling a real holo-table right in front of you feels extremely immersive and very “Star Wars.” The story mode also surprised me much more than I expected.

But while playing, I couldn’t stop thinking about how massive the potential of this game is and how it only goes halfway. Honestly, if the races had been made as full VR experiences instead of focusing mainly on mixed reality/tabletop gameplay, this could easily have been one of the best Meta Quest games and probably the best Star Wars VR game ever made.

Imagine experiencing those races from inside the vehicle at full speed with complete VR immersion. That would have been insane. The foundation is already there: the universe, the visuals, the sound design, the feeling of truly being inside Star Wars… but it feels like the developers never fully committed to turning it into the bigger and more ambitious game it could have been.

Even so, I genuinely loved the experience and don’t regret playing it at all. The reason it leaves such a strong impression is because you can clearly see how close it is to becoming something truly incredible.

Is it gorgeous…yes. But could it be so much more… yes. It’s like Ilm management said make mixed reality Star Wars. We all want first person pod racing and flight. How about oculus version of Squadrons???

First of all, I was shocked seeing the credits after an hour give or take. For 20€, I was expecting way more. And by saying an hour, it's not only the gameplay but the cutscenes as well...

For the game, it is fun and the mixed reality is interesting. The story mode is nice but very easy with no thinking or riddles. It could have been more challenging when it comes on repairing the pod and looking for the pieces.

The racing mode has 4-5 different circuits as in the story mode and there you can choose other characters so there is a little more replayability. It would be nice if there were different story modes for those characters as well.

The 3rd mode is the room personalization with decoration and no real interest.

It’s a great game, but you play it once then it just sits in your library and you forget about it. I also would of loved to see more first person view and then I would love the pod racing to be in first person instead of just playing it on the table top

Now I got this for £5.59 as I owned all the other games, so how can I complain, I know it’s short but at the price I paid this is a super bargain, it’s definitely not a £15.99 game.. obviously the licensing. But I’m having a lot of fun.

reviews.review_title: fun experience for Star Wars fans
reviews.review_description:
-the story adventure, was interesting. I would have liked that whole thing to be fleshed out more though and been much longer, or just the whole game be the story mode but a lot more expanded.

-the podracing gameplay is ok but not an ideal setup, I got better at driving with it by the final story race, but it still did not feel like good control

-the playset stuff is really cool, I am personally not into sandbox gameplay, however I enjoyed all the tools and details there when I tried it for a bit; it's great if you love that type of gameplay and love Star Wars

reviews.review_title: Worthy for Star Wars and VR fans.
reviews.review_description: Strong Story, but not a major plot point for the Star Wars universe. This game is a good experiment in VR and MR, but not as strong as you'd want it to be in all areas. It's weird going between VR and MR, third person and first person... but it's an interesting, high production value product in a world a lot of people want to see more of. Look up my full review on YouTube from VR HERO.

5 stars bc it’s not fair this game is rated so low. Everyone holds Lucas arts to an unrealistic high standard. Only paid $6 bc I owned the bundle it was on sale with. Great play for an Amazing price for May the 4th!!!

reviews.review_title: Doing a lot of new things
reviews.review_description: I had to write a review because I thought this was a really unique game experimenting with a lot of modes, and I'd love to see more games like it. Really innovative story mode, where some of it you watch on a virtual "table", and then sometimes you go in full immersion. And then there's the racing. When I watched the videos and read the reviews, I was very hesitant on purchasing this, but I just finished the story mode and I had to write a review. Something that had me on the fence was that the pod racing scenes looked lackluster in the videos, and it looked like you didn't have enough forward-vision to see what was coming. That ended up not being a concern at all. The racing in story mode is great, and then you can do repeat racing of those courses with different vehicles. Very fun. I also liked the play set mode, where you can size and pose the different characters in your own space. JUST REALLY COOL usability and game modes, and would love to see more games continue to push AR and various game modes like this one did.

I just don't get it. We finally get a VR podracing game, and they choose to make the racing portion of the game tabletop? This could have been something really special. Instead, it's something I'll probably play a few times just because it's Star Wars, then forget I have it in my library. The worst part is they give you a taste of what could have been in the VR portion of the game just to switch to a tabletop view for the part that should be in VR. I'm incredibly disappointed. That said, it's not a terrible game. That just makes it harder. You can see the potential that was squandered. Frankly, I'm baffled.
I wanna see more story and playtime. Also first person view in race would be great thing ^_^

Different parts of the game are presented in different ways. Some parts are like a third-person top-down view looking at these characters running around on the mixed reality tabletop in front of you, some parts are a more immersive VR experience, and some parts are pod racing with a top-down third person view. 

There were some small parts where I had to find an object and figure out how to use it to repair a pod racer, and the gameplay in that part reminded me of Lone Echo a little.  I didn’t like those parts involving fixing the pod racer.  However  the  racing parts, the cinematic and cutscenes, and the story experience were all very enjoyable.

It was really an amazing experience to see the Star Wars story and cinematic experience in 3D.

reviews.review_title: Worth it for Star Wars fans.
reviews.review_description:
🚨Full review available on Gamertag VR YouTube channel🚨

Overall this is cool tech demo for mixed reality on Quest3 and does indeed flex some graphical muscle in the virtual reality parts. It’s got all the signs of a big budget vr release and for $20 it’s not exactly breaking the bank and there is at least 4-5 hours of content plus online leader boards BUT… I’m not disappointed in the what we have, more that it could have been so much more. It’s clear playing in mixed reality is taxing for the Quest 3 and I do wonder how a true full virtual reality podracing experience might have been and that’s where I feel some disappointment. Do I recommend Star Wars Beyond Victory? Yes for Star Wars fans it’s a no brainer and cool experience, for everyone else heed what I’ve said that this is a tech demo, a vertical slice of something cool that you’ll soon forget you own after 4/5 hours.

The VR portion plays like Vader Immortal in that it holds your hand and tells you to fetch this, repair that, etc. but it doesn’t have any gameplay in VR that makes up for it. Choosing to race in mixed reality was a terrible decision as it’s not enjoyable at all. To finally get a VR podracing game and not be able to race in VR is criminal. 

It has a small story, but the lack of freedom makes it a chore. The planet is another middle of nowhere experience with nothing interesting to see. That and the racing mode are my primary reasons for such a low score. I hope ILM make better decisions on their next game. 

As an aside, it’d be nice to play in the Clone Wars era for once. I’m sick of the Empire and First Order settings. I want more of the space experience and war part of Star Wars instead of these small-scale landlocked barren planet experiences.

Star Wars: Beyond Victory is a fantastic podracing game. While I understand this isn’t the Star Wars game many fans were expecting, as a mixed reality experience it's exceptional. It delivers beautiful graphics, smooth gameplay/controls, a compelling story, and plenty of content to enjoy, making it thoroughly fun and engaging. The title showcases exactly what the Meta Quest 3’s mixed reality is capable of, and in my opinion it’s a must-have for any Star Wars fan.

What a fun experience.  I love the mixed reality combined with full vr.  Playing on a mixed reality board and being able to watch cutscenes from any angle in my living room is so much fun, this is exactly what I want out of a vr game.

Beyond expectations.

Alas, commenters unanimously and universally write about the lack of first person view, similar to Need For Speed No Limits on Android tablet. No limits except for the lack of a bumper camera.

"It is nothing without the loop."  The Simpsons
It is nothing without the first person POV.

If you want the pod racing first person view experience, play Dawn of Jets and fly with the canopy down and the volume down otherwise you'll get a voice always reminding you, " You are flying too fast with the canopy down!"

This app is profound. I would give it a hundred stars out of ten. The adventure mode was mind blowing. I really felt like I was actually in the world of the movie that I saw in 1977 and wondered if Luke Skywalker was somewhere around.
Adventure mode. The dioramas are incredible. I remember when I was young visiting friends and relatives who had more money and the best Star Wars toys. With the dioramas I feel like I also have the best Star Wars toys. The figures seem like pricey high end large size Star Wars Action figurines only found in the most upper class toy stores in Europe.
I don't understand the opprobrium that some have expressed regarding adventure mode. It is the most extravagant over the top Star Wars experience that I have ever had. 
The mixed reality models level. When I set up C3PO, R2D2, the storm trooper, again, the massive Star Wars vibe. So much so I was still shaking after turning off the app. 
The racing mode gave me massive motion sickness. The only remedy is to scale it down to the smallest size. For some intangible reason, even though I was confused and motion sick, I still placed first in the pod race. 
The team that made this app are geniuses. Each and every one of them. 

The force will be with you, always.

reviews.review_title: A fun interactive story for SW fans
reviews.review_description: Yes, we can all admit that it would be better if it was first person pod racing. However, once you get past that, its still a fun game. Its not all in MR. There's a fair amount of VR scenes as well. Its basically an interactive movie where you play as the main character, and I really like that about it. There's a big mix of gameplay styles. First person, third person, arcade, puzzle solving, etc. Overall, Im really enjoying it. I think anyone who likes StarWars and narrative games, will enjoy this.
I wish they would make more games like this the whole AR table look third person view is not done enough . I love it this is in my top vr games. Please need more like this

reviews.review_title: Star Wars: Beyond Victory — A Tech D
reviews.review_description:
This is a technology showcase, not a game. Beyond Victory is a prime example of developers prioritizing a Mixed Reality (MR) gimmick over the essential pillars that made the previous Star Wars VR titles successful — namely, tight pacing, immediate immersion, and clear purpose.

The Fatal Design Regression:

• Technology-First, User-Experience-Last: 

The entire structure feels loosely built around the novelty of showcasing MR capabilities. The story, the objectives, and the action sequences appear to be mere afterthoughts—thin scaffolding supporting the tech demo. This approach immediately breaks the sense of urgency and magic we expect from a Star Wars adventure.

• Immersion-Breaking Friction: 

Instead of being pulled into a galaxy far, far away, the confusing initial objectives and slow pace feel like mandatory steps to activate the next MR feature. This replaces narrative drive with technical obligation, leading directly to the feeling of being "at a loss as to what to do."

• The Comfort Cost: 

My experience of dizziness and discomfort upon making progress is likely tied to this MR focus. When the priority shifts to blending the virtual world with the real one, any technical imperfections or forced movement can quickly lead to disorientation. This is an unacceptable comfort failure for a major franchise VR release.

Verdict: 

Not the Sequel Fans Waited For
If you loved the previous Star Wars VR games for their focused action and compelling immersion, be warned: this is not the natural next chapter. It’s a pacing mismatch where the novelty of the technical feat quickly wears off, leaving behind a slow, confusing, and ultimately joyless experience.

This is a tech demonstration with a Star Wars license slapped on. Unless you are only interested in a rudimentary MR showcase, I strongly recommend waiting for a deep sale and significant updates.

I gave it three stars because what's here is so polished and pretty fun. But the story is super short, and unlike Vader Immortal you don't get to do anything fun in it. Mostly you watch really well done cut scenes. And the racing only has four tracks, this is where it could have shined with all the cool planets that pod racing takes place on. I would add the table needs to be longer so we can see upcoming turns and obsticles. I was kinda disappointed.

With one of the largest Ip's in history they decided to make a 3rd person rc controller style pod racer game. Smh  a remake of pod racer in vr would have been awesome. This game was boring and fast. You can beat it within an hour. I didn't care for it and my 7 year old even thought it was boring. Definitely refunding.

Should of been a $9.99 game because it is short. I thought I would be racing other people online, but unless I'm missing something you can't do that. I thought I would be racing, getting credits and building better/custom pod racer with more capabilities. 

Not a fan of the 3rd person scenes. Just keep me in VR. Pod racing on the virtual table is okay. I get that that helps with rendering speeds.

Buggy mission completion messages. They where so far down, I couldn't even read them.

reviews.review_title: This is a 20 dollar tech demo.
reviews.review_description: Although they did a great job with the story, this is a tech demo at best. The main story played out within maybe 2 hours or less of game play. I thought the replayabilty would be in the pod racing with maybe an upgrade system and getting new racers like in the classic pod racing game. Nope just a chance to replay the races that you played in the game with the same vehicle that was in that segment, for better times. And the playset aspect gets old after the first couple times you use it. 20 dollars for this game was a huge rip off.

reviews.review_title: Zero Flaws, All Fun, and Great Story!
reviews.review_description: This game is a genuine masterpiece, and I absolutely love it! It features a nice story with exciting sky-high racing and heist stakes, with a great cast of characters. I especially love listening to Sebulba’s dialogue. I encountered no glitches and no motion sickness, and I was even able to adjust the main character’s arms for a more comfortable setup while gaming in bed. This is one of my favorite games.

So the toy box is awesome. I'd like to say I felt like a kid again, but I never really stopped being one. The adventure was great. I don't mind that the pod racing is in MR, not VR. Though I agree with other reviewers, the choice to race in full VR would be much better. My biggest problem with the game is, as other reviewers have also pointed out, you can't see far enough ahead to judge turns and obstacles. Now, since they appear to have hinged any progress in the adventure mode on coming 1st, it relies on you grinding over and over again until you memorize every turn and obstacle on the course. It seems to me that this means, unless you win the race, no more story, and no more toys for your toy box. If anyone knows a way around this, please let me know, and I will change my review to 5 stars!

developer_response.body: Please reach out to the support team at https://support.starwars.com or support@starwars.com to see if they can help.

reviews.review_title: Not what I expected at all
reviews.review_description: This game is 10/10 100% recommend you will be amazed

it’s fun, but honestly, I really miss being able to see the horizon like in the Star Wars Racer PC game. It really bothers me during gameplay. I think it's okay to be MR, but it would need to extend further ahead to allow for a bit more anticipation, at the very least. A VR mode, though — that would be fantastic. I believe it's possible to create a VR experience similar to a hoverbike game without causing discomfort.

EDIT: The game only has 3 or 4 different tracks, the story is extremely short — this is ridiculous! What a missed opportunity!

I knew long before buying this that I was going to be interested mostly in the story mode. My expectation and standard for Star Wars VR games is limited to the game’s ability to allow me (even for a brief moment) to live in the Star Wars universe.

Sure, it would have been great to get 1st-person pod racing and not have to lose the immersive environment, but I am still very happy with my purchase.

From a company which has created some incredible and immersive experiences, this is an embarrassment. I purchased it knowing it was not full vr podracing game. But the main game is a little over an hour. Would it have been that hard to give everyone what they want and make a full vr podracing mode?  I get it you were probably paid by meta to make a mixed reality game for the quest 3 and had certain benchmarks to hit but man this could have been a really awesome game with more story and a full VR mode.

I’m a big Star Wars fan, and loved the Vader series, wish they’d release more galaxy’s edge
Content, even still play pinball. All have the feel
and ethos of the Star Wars universe. 


So I bought this as soon as it came out and …. Meh. There are nods to Star Wars but really none of the feel. The constant switching between immersive VR and diorama seems pointless and annoying. The pod racing sequences are a distraction and clunky. 

 Roll on Galaxy’s Edge III

Feelings about game: The game is a cool concept… Honestly at one point in the beginning I was like wow Ive never seen mixed reality implemented like this - this is really cool. Soon that feeling slowly went away as I got used to it, and then I was stuck with the gameplay - which was just okay. The racing part was kinda fun, but was maybe 5% of the gameplay and the game is really short as it is. Game would have been more complete with more races, rewards for races, and then ability to purchase upgrades for your pod and being the mechanic to make those upgrades on your pod. As a side note I will say that the story was really well written, engaging, and fun.

Where I think it went wrong: In the end they failed to make the game with the user experience in mind, as no one wants a podracer game in mixed reality…. Especially one that only has like 3 races and then the game is over… It seems like they forced to make this game a mixed reality title, when a podracing game shouldve always been made in virtual reality…. If they really wanted to make a mixed reality title I think they could have chosen more compatible storylines that would better fit a mixed reality title to revolve a game around. Which is a real shame because this studio has made so many great games, and my personal favorite way back in 2020 (Tales from a Galaxys Edge). It is still a really good game for what it strives to do, but like I said, the whole concept and idea of the game was the wrong one. The developers are some of the best, so I trust them to listen to the feedback and come out firing with their next release.

Many VR reviewers/influencers have trashed this game. While I can agree with some points, there is some fun to be had in innthis title. First of all the good: The MR elements are neat, especially in the playground mode, but if you've experienced MR before, its nothing special. The story and voice acting are good. Now the bad: The graphics, while good, still don't hold a candle to the Vader Immortal trilogy. I booted up Vader Immortal episode 2, and I found myself thinking 'this looks amazing!'. Beyond Victory? Meh? Another bad thing is the game is short, I mean really short. You could finish it in one sitting, with no incentive to play it again. The MR pod racing is NOT fun. Between your tiny spec of a racer, to the short draw distance due to the holo table view, its more frustrating than fun. Theres an option to replay the races, which Im sure nobody will want to use. I know it takes resources and people to make a quality game, I get that...but this could have been cheaper, and released in episodes like Vader Immortal. I had no problem paying $10 per episode, but $20 for such a short game? All in all, I DID enjoy this, but it could have been so much more.

game is not worth it at all 
No tutorial just instantly put into a pod race with no idea how to play. 90% of gameplay is top down view, extremely boring and constant switching between first and top down view. The top down view makes the game very unimmersive, and makes you feel disconnected from the game and world. This game would be great if it was one view or the other, imagine old racing in first person like a normal vr game instead of watching a boring dirt road from above. 
Do not recommend unless you like a very slow game that doesn’t play well.

reviews.review_title: POD racing frustrating / story mode cool
reviews.review_description:
Joshua Parria for I, the pod racing has been more frustration than enjoyment. Volo’s pod even though very small and light, can’t keep up with most of the other pods. Even the heavy larger ones, without spaming its roll special. However, doing this constantly gets you killed rolling into the landscape or crashing into another pod which instead kills you and not them. So basically everything destroys you. 

I was disappointed that some of the pod racers aren’t from the phantom menace besides Sebulba but as a mentor and not as a rider. The lack of draw distance in front of the pod racer is not enough but does add an element of challenge and quick wits. Honestly it’s just pure stubbornness than any actual enjoyment that keeps me continually playing the pod race. 

However, ironically what sold me is the story part of the game. It’s very cool how it transitions from mixed reality 3rd person tabletop view to POV VR mode when entering buildings.

reviews.review_title: Knocked it out of the park
reviews.review_description: The graphics the game play  the story 10 minutes in sold . My Vr has been brought back to life cleaned the cob webs off for this game . Thank you for making a great game if you are a Star Wars fan must have they did a great job mixing first person third person pod racing and exploration the audio the visuals wow thank you 🎩🎩🎩 can’t wait for more to come about to go enjoy this till my headset and batteries die lol happy gaming

I love the graphics on all the Star Wars series (brought the previous Star Wars as well). But I'm not a fan of shooting games, and they had a lot of that - I suck at shooting games LOL. This one allows me to explore, figure stuff out, and even bring Star Wars into my house as miniature or as huge character 3D objects that TALK! Curious people will love this! Please do more like this....possibly adding some puzzles. Thank you to the devs!

Absolutely brilliant! Briliant story ,The use of mixed reality and full vr in the story is awesome, and some unexpected jump scares in it ,i played with a constant smile on my face ,feels like your in a star wars movie ,and the pod racing feels amazing to play .....best star wars game I have ever played ,and in fact,so far one of my favourite games ive ever played period!

reviews.review_title: It’s all about the Toy Box mode
reviews.review_description: If you like/collect action figures this game is a must buy. The Toy Box mode is exactly what action figure collectors have been waiting for in AR/VR. It’s still fairly simple(it’s brand new), but if the developers see the long term potential in this mode and keep adding more characters ,vehicles, environments, items, then I think this will be one of the Must Have Quest Apps! Note to Developers: a “stop motion animation” mode with recordable videos you can share/upload would be a home run here. 4 out of 5 only because there’s SO MUCH POTENTIAL in the toy box mode, I want to see more content before awarding 5 stars.

reviews.review_title: Just play one of the old Podrace games
reviews.review_description:
The podracing itself is pretty bad. You can barely see more than a few meters in front of the Pod, the controls are wobbly and the way the Pod interacts with terrain is just... weird. Hard to describe. Since this is the main part of the "game", it should be at least fun but it really isn't.

The story mode is very passive. Most of the time you just stand there listening to the dialogue. Even if action goes down and people shoot at you and your friends you just passively stand around.

If you walk aroune you do that in third person most of the time qhile - just as in the podracing - only a few meters of your surroundings are rendered. If you entwe a curscene the game changes in between camera positions whoch is just really distracting

I really liked Vader Immortal and Tales from the Galaxy's edge, but this thing here is just bad. sorry.

the "playroom" feature is also a bit buggy. You can decorate your room (not sire why you would do that rhough) but if you come back the next time it didn't accurately sace the positions so things are at the wrong place.

Just play Episode I Racer or Racer Revenge. Those are as much VRbracing games as this one (not at all) and they are actually fun to play
Alright hear me out... Forget the podracing, go play the Playset game mode. Endless fun if you love Star Wars toys
I always appreciate an in depth experience as a game. highly recommended!

The story campaign is really good and I enjoyed it a lot, but it is very short 
The gameplay for the pod racing is 50-50 
The environment ￼and the pods are good 
But not being able to see ahead of you very far￼ but only a over head view makes me not want to drive very fast which is not what you want on a racing game 
.￼have I used  the haste haste positions in dungeon of eternity and I do fine. It should’ve been a VR game.
But also mixed reality adds nothing
It doesn’t interact with the environment or surround your environment. It’s just an adjustable table kind of board game.
So the mixed reality part is mostly a gimmick since it doesn’t cover or interact with your real environment
.the graphics are great 
Being able to look at the models fun ￼

reviews.review_title: Holotable sets a new standard for AR
reviews.review_description:
I really like the holotable - you can resize it and move it around, then reach into the world and pan it around, zoom in/out and spin it. The world also gets nicely sliced at the edges with a cool holographic effect. I wish there was some interpolation to the hand movements to smooth things out. When your character moves the world scrolls, it's kinds like the table is a monitor laying flat on the table but the graphics pop out of it or sink down in it. Oh I also wish I could dip my head into the table to see things up close but instead it vanishes as I cross the plane of the table. The races are cool but I wish I could see further ahead - when the map goes below ground level if you look at the right angle you can see twice as far ahead so i wonder if they could put a portal at the end of the table to allow you to see further ahead as an option - or make the table longer lol

Anyway it's great value for money, highly polished experience, everyone wishes it was longer but then it would cost more - i hope they share some info about how they do the mesh slicing its some really cool tech and I want more table top rpg games in VR

Update:

I'm still not happy with the guided linear gameplay, but the graphics have really grown on me. 

I compared it back to back with Galaxy's Edge to refresh my memory and the sharper resolution and more detailed models and textures are obvious now.

As the game progresses there are some spectacular visual moments. The characters are extremely detailed and very well modeled. The game does the Quest 3 proud.

The pod racing is fun and playing from a 3rd person view on the holographic table is interesting in an old school video game kinda way.

I have hit a few glitches but I'm not going to wait for the inevitable updates because just like the previous Star Wars games on the Quest I'll be playing through this more than once.

1st Impressions:

Star Wars VR games have always been enjoyable for me and I expect this one will be as well but there are a few issues right off the bat.

In the MR mode your guardian is still active, but this may be a problem imposed on developers by Meta since every mixed MR/VR game has this issue. My guess is that Meta doesn't allow developers to switch the guardian on and off during a game. Having a guardian in MR is a real immersion breaker.

The initial tutorial was a bit frustrating. After it showed me how to move the holographic table around it seemed to get stuck before I realized it insisted I move the table in all directions to proceed.

The pod racing tutorial also is a bit confusing.

The adventure mode didn't make a great first impression when it transitioned to VR. Being a Quest 3 only game I was looking forward to an upgrade in visuals over Galaxy's Edge, a game initially developed for the Quest 1. Unfortunately it looks pretty much like Galaxy's Edge with slightly higher resolution.  For example there are no real time shadows just simple fuzzy blobs directly under moving objects. (Maybe they'll release a Quest 3 update later. ;) )

Luckily it still has that beautiful ILM art direction so despite the dated rendering tech, it's still a very good looking game.

The adventure so far is another simple fetch quest / guided object interaction game like Galaxy's Edge so it should be fun but I really wish you could turn off the hints and repetitive voice over prompts.

Unfortunately unlike Galaxy's Edge there doesn't seem to be an inventory system. Objects vanish once used, then sometimes reappear later as needed.

Another missing feature that Galaxy's Edge has are difficulty settings. The only difficulty level is the "Tell me what to do every step of the way" difficulty setting. This is even more frustrating given that there's a hint button on the controller. If players can choose to ask for hints why give them automatically?

Being told what to do instead of being left to figure things out on your own is by far the worst aspect of this game and it's the reason I've given it a provisional 3 stars. The score may go up after I complete the game.

I’ve always been a big fan of the Star Wars Quest titles. The stories are interesting, and the realtime immersive experience keeps me engaged.

Star Wars Beyond Victory is made up of three parts:

Adventure Mode: Follows the character Volo in both mixed reality and full VR, delivering a compelling narrative that blends real-time immersion with cinematic storytelling. The characters are cool, and the sets really make me feel like I’m in a Star Wars movie.

Arcade Mode: A tabletop-style pod racing AR experience that captures the excitement of racing through Star Wars environments. Pod Racing is enjoyable, but I feel it would be better as a full VR, first-person experience. Still enjoyable. Maybe devs are working on another POV Podracing game. 

Playset Mode: Lets you play with posable action figures, adding a tactile layer to the game. Being able to scale action figures to full size and place them in my living room is fantastic. The Stormtrooper’s sound bites are hilarious. 

Highly recommended for Star Wars fans.

It had me saying wow a lot but the story was so short and there are only 4 races to redo, meaning the one's you do during the story are the "races". Nothing compared to the other 2 games you put out. Disappointed to say the least

I have only played this game (adventure mode) for 30 minutes and would already rate it a 5. It has been a blast so far. What a unique experience to bounce back and forth from various scenes to either immersive or tabletop. Very creative. The voice acting and writing has been top notch - the actors are super compelling too. All great. 

I really appreciate the attention to detail. The little things really set games like this apart.  The immersive part has the feel of previous Star Wars games (Vader + tales) and the tabletop reminds me of Lego brick tales. It’s great. 

The rest of the game could be garbage and I’d still rate it a 5 because of the great experience so far.

Loved the parts in VR and the story that went along with it. MR was good but not the best medium to use for the pod races, it would be nice to see what is approaching and was really a missed opportunity there.

Would pay more for a proper game like tales from the edge but will settle for this as I love star wars.

Overall it's OK. I'm a huge Star Wars fan so I love that aspect. The campaign is good so far. I'm not thrilled with the pod racing. The play set is fun for a while.
All that said, now can we have some more TFTGE content for the Q3?

I really enjoyed this. I'm not a big Star wars fan but liked the gameplay. I use the quest optimizer to get a bit more out of the graphics. Looked really detailed when you get close to the characters. Took me about 5 hours to complete the story. Good quality for the money on this game. 
If the developers read this. I just got a bhaptics jacket, might you be able to apply it into the game if you are still tweaking any bugs or updates.

I really love this game. Best game I own  on the quest. The only problem is it is way too short. I would love this to be a full length game. It ends just as you start really getting into it. So sad I finished it.

Really cool
It feels like HotWheels racing
If you played BlazeRush on Quest, is something similar in the racing part
But there is other aspects I never seen in a racing game
The story mode was really great and definitely worth experiencing it.
...very, very short game. You can finish it in 2h tops. Combination of VR and MR is not bad. I hope they will make some updates for this to make game worth money.

It’s great.
Went in with low expectations… but this delivers in spades! 
The gameplay is fun, and the graphics are actually excellent!
More games based on the prequels please!

Episodes 1-6 are StarWars!
That other poodoo doesn’t count.

The playset is definitely the best part; I'm giving it my rating. The racing needs some improvement... racing like this is awful; I've been preferring to race around the house floor with the playset's ships and pods.

Devs, please add a photo mode to the playset; that would be amazing.

reviews.review_title: Well Executed Star Wars Mixed Reality
reviews.review_description: Kudos to the team at ILM. This is one of the best and most unique mixed reality experiences with seamless VR and table top mixed reality experiences. The characters are well voiced and the graphical fidelity is really good. Disregard the negative reviews. The style of the game and character models is what definitely stands out! Hope for future stories of the galaxy

Not completed yet, as I like to take my time and savour all the little details that are hidden in this game but from what I have played so far,  the graphics are some of the best I have seen on the Quest. The characters looks amazing. I love the swapping from MR to VR. It is the first game I have played that does that and it works really well. 
A see lot of people moaning about the draw distance on the pod race, but I don't see the issue with it. Pod racing is about quick reactions, surely, just like in Phantom Menace.
So far a great game and reasonably priced for a Star Wars game.
I would like to see more Star Wars games like this as we need more big brand games in VR and less of the stupid monkey tag games (they are just awful)!

reviews.review_title: Campaign is short  but worth it
reviews.review_description: Story could be longer (1,5 hours), becouse I love these Star Wars games on Quest :) I would love moreee. 3rd person parts looks rly cool in mix reality (I can imagine play Diablo like this) .. but mixed reality races and mixed reality dioramas with star wars persons and stuffs, I  don't need them at all... But I could not wait :) 10-15€ would fit more
I love, This Game, it’s very Fantastic. The Devs are make a very good Game.  That make very Fun for play in the Mixed Reality ￼￼￼⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍😎😎😎😎😎😎😎😎😎😎

Great fun and if nothing else having a full sized R2 D2 in my room in Mixed Reality is very cool..
All we need now is the "Death Star Run" as an addon in the same mode as the Pod Racing and that will be the icing on the cake :)
It's a bit odd going back and forth between VR and MR but the gameplay is excellent. I dont see myself using the playset mode but nice addition. If you like star wars youll like this 👌

reviews.review_title: This game gives you so much
reviews.review_description: This game much better than you expect based on its price. Beautifull graphic, good story, and exellent animations. Respect for Indrustrial Light and Magic.
I really liked the game and was having fun then all of a sudden it was over :-( Campaign was way to short!

Great game i love the idea i guess im not a racer been a month and i still can win the first race
so i guess im done

Absolutely love it. Best new VR game. I’ve seen in a long time few little glitches, but you can easily work around them 
Worth twice the price
If you are a fan of Star Wars at any age this is a great game. Story mode is short but for $20, it feels fair. I hope they do more with this.
Play it like micro cars or Mario Kart. It is fun, and i loved the game for being AR, so i can see my soroundings. If you love Star Wars it is a must! 🤖💕

The mix of full and augmented vr was spot on. The voice actors nailed it, give them raises. Itd be fun to have more chapters of the story in the future and custom podracers. Definitely worth the money imo
The ONLY thing I need in a pod racer game is FPV...
The visuals to the story mode is all perfect. I love it so much.  we need more starwars games like this.
Playsets are the coolest.
I try playing and i gave all permissions but it just puts back and white flashes on my screen

developer_response.body: Please reach out to our support team at https://support.starwars.com or support@starwars.com to see if we can help.

reviews.review_description: I have not finished it but it is pretty good I really don't have any complaints
Fun
真的很无聊

This is the 1st time mixed reality gaming comes together completely organically. It would be almost a perfect game if they could make the switch to in-game and mix reality smoother like waling through a door. Other then that, if your a fan of starwars or good vr mechanics and story. I think we may have a game of the year vr contender. I play maby 3 of these games a year in vr so it's a real reason to keeps and use your quest.
